reference.title: Debugging Playbook
reference.shortdesc: Systematic approach to isolating DITA-OT build failures across the three stages: preprocessing, transformation, and packaging.

ul.li: Wrong content (missing steps, wrong links, unresolved keyrefs) → preprocessing issue
Correct content, wrong markup → XSLT transform issue
Correct HTML, missing CSS/JS/images → packaging/copy issue

section.title: If you suspect preprocess2 vs preprocess mismatch
section.p: Check whether the transtype uses map-first preprocessing:

ul.li: HTML5 uses map-first preprocessing as of 4.2

section.p: If your extension points aren't firing, it may be because step-specific hooks aren't available in preprocess2.
section.note: The official docs explicitly warn that the internal order of preprocessing steps can change between versions. If you're hooking inside preprocess, assume you must maintain it across OT upgrades.

strow.stentry: Content that "should be excluded" still appears
filtering config not applied early enough

section.title: Reproduce in Docker to isolate environment issues
section.p: If a build fails only on CI or only on a coworker's machine, run the same build using a pinned Docker image:
This eliminates OS-level differences, Java version mismatches, and locally installed plugin conflicts.

section.title: Use strict processing mode for quality gates
section.p: If your builds silently succeed with missing references, you ship broken docs. Add strict mode to CI:
